name: Spectre

documentation.readme: Spectre is an Android app for observing and interacting with the wireless environment around you. It monitors signals from Bluetooth devices, Wi-Fi access points, cellular towers, and GNSS satellites. Spectre also features a growing set of pentesting tools, constrained by Android's security model.
usage_sections: Installation: Minimum supported version: Android 12 (API 31) It's good practice to verify the APK's signing certificate against the CERT.sha256 fingerprint before installing. Once Spectre is installed, grant the permissions it requests - they power the scanners and nothing else. The app has no

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web, cloud, iots] in the Reconnaissance category. Fits when the engagement needs: ![Banner](metadata/en-US/images/featureGraphic.png).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Spectre is an Android app for observing and interacting with the wireless environment around you. It monitors signals from Bluetooth devices, Wi-Fi access points, cellular towers, and GNSS satellites. Spectre also features a growing set of pentesting tools, constrained by Android's security model.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `Spectre` from its upstream repository, then run `Spectre --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.
